message:
✨ Update Armada install mission to use Armada Operator (#2071)

- Switch primary install method to Armada Operator (recommended by maintainers)
- Bump version from v0.20.36 to v0.20.40
- Add Armada Operator Helm install + CRD-based component deployment
- Add kube-prometheus-stack to dependencies
- Add armadactl CLI installation and configuration
- Add Lookout UI access instructions
- Add PriorityClass setup step
- Add operator-specific troubleshooting (cert-manager, Lookout, prometheus)
- Add 'operator' to installMethods and operator URL to sourceUrls
- Expand tags with batch-scheduling and multi-cluster

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -6,43 +6,55 @@
   "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
   "mission": {
     "title": "Install and Configure Armada on Kubernetes",
-    "description": "Armada is a CNCF sandbox project for multi-cluster batch job scheduling. It enables efficient queuing, scheduling, and management of high-throughput compute workloads across multiple Kubernetes clusters, ideal for enterprises with large-scale batch processing needs.",
+    "description": "Armada is a CNCF sandbox project for multi-cluster batch job scheduling. It enables efficient queuing, scheduling, and management of high-throughput compute workloads across multiple Kubernetes clusters. This mission covers both the recommended Armada Operator method and the manual Helm-based approach.",
     "type": "deploy",
     "status": "completed",
     "steps": [
       {
-        "title": "Install prerequisites",
-        "description": "Armada requires several dependencies to be installed first. Ensure the following are running in your cluster:

- **PostgreSQL** — persistent storage for job metadata
- **Redis** — caching layer
- **Apache Pulsar** — message broker for inter-component communication

You can install these via Helm:
```bash
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm install postgresql bitnami/postgresql --namespace armada --create-namespace
helm install redis bitnami/redis --namespace armada
helm repo add apache https://pulsar.apache.org/charts
helm install pulsar apache/pulsar --namespace armada
```"
+        "title": "Install the Armada Operator (recommended)",
+        "description": "The Armada Operator is the recommended way to install Armada. It uses Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) to manage the lifecycle of Armada services.

```bash
helm repo add gresearch https://g-research.github.io/charts
helm repo update
helm install armada-operator gresearch/armada-operator --namespace armada-system --create-namespace
```

Verify the operator is running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace armada-system
```"
       },
       {
-        "title": "Add the Armada Helm repository",
-        "description": "Add the G-Research Helm repository:
```bash
helm repo add gresearch https://g-research.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```"
+        "title": "Install Armada dependencies",
+        "description": "Armada requires several external dependencies:

- **PostgreSQL** — persistent storage for job metadata
- **Redis** — caching layer
- **Apache Pulsar** — message broker for inter-component communication
- **kube-prometheus-stack** — monitoring

Install via Helm:
```bash
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo add apache https://pulsar.apache.org/charts
helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update

helm install postgresql bitnami/postgresql --namespace armada --create-namespace
helm install redis bitnami/redis --namespace armada
helm install pulsar apache/pulsar --namespace armada
helm install kube-prometheus-stack prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring --create-namespace
```

Wait for all dependencies to be ready:
```bash
kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app.kubernetes.io/name=postgresql --namespace armada --timeout=300s
kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app.kubernetes.io/name=redis --namespace armada --timeout=300s
```"
       },
       {
-        "title": "Install Armada server using Helm",
-        "description": "Install the Armada server. You will need to configure the values to point to your PostgreSQL, Redis, and Pulsar instances:
```bash
helm install armada-server gresearch/armada --namespace armada --version 0.20.36
```

For a complete deployment, you also need the executor (per worker cluster) and scheduler:
```bash
helm install armada-executor gresearch/armada-executor --namespace armada
helm install armada-scheduler gresearch/armada-scheduler --namespace armada
```"
+        "title": "Deploy Armada components via CRDs",
+        "description": "With the Armada Operator running and dependencies ready, deploy Armada components using Custom Resources:

```bash
kubectl create namespace armada
kubectl apply -n armada -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/armadaproject/armada-operator/main/dev/quickstart/armada-crs.yaml
```

This deploys:
- **Armada Server** — API server for job submission
- **Armada Executor** — runs jobs on worker clusters
- **Armada Scheduler** — schedules jobs across clusters
- **Armada Lookout** — web UI for monitoring jobs (exposed on NodePort 30000)
- **Armada Lookout Ingester** — ingests events for the Lookout UI
- **Armada Event Ingester** — processes job events

Verify all components are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace armada
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply default PriorityClass",
+        "description": "Armada requires a default PriorityClass for all jobs:

```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/armadaproject/armada-operator/main/dev/quickstart/priority-class.yaml
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install armadactl CLI",
+        "description": "Download the armadactl binary for interacting with the Armada API:

```bash
# Download the latest armadactl for your platform from:
# https://github.com/armadaproject/armada/releases/latest

# Create a config file:
mkdir -p ~/.armada
cat > ~/.armadactl.yaml << EOF
currentContext: main
contexts:
  main:
    armadaUrl: localhost:30002
EOF
```

Test the connection:
```bash
armadactl create queue example
armadactl submit dev/quickstart/example-job.yaml
```"
       },
       {
         "title": "Verify the installation",
-        "description": "Check that the Armada components are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace armada
```

You should see pods for armada-server, armada-executor, and armada-scheduler."
+        "description": "Check that all Armada components are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace armada
kubectl get pods --namespace armada-system
```

Access the Lookout UI at `http://localhost:30000` to monitor jobs.

You should see pods for armada-server, armada-executor, armada-scheduler, armada-lookout, and the ingester components."
       }
     ],
     "resolution": {
-      "summary": "A successful installation shows the Armada server, executor, and scheduler pods running in the 'armada' namespace, with connectivity to PostgreSQL, Redis, and Pulsar.",
+      "summary": "A successful installation shows the Armada Operator running in 'armada-system', all Armada components (server, executor, scheduler, lookout, ingesters) running in the 'armada' namespace, and the Lookout UI accessible on port 30000.",
       "codeSnippets": [
-        "helm repo add gresearch https://g-research.github.io/charts
helm repo update",
-        "helm install armada-server gresearch/armada --namespace armada --create-namespace --version 0.20.36",
+        "helm repo add gresearch https://g-research.github.io/charts
helm install armada-operator gresearch/armada-operator --namespace armada-system --create-namespace",
+        "kubectl apply -n armada -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/armadaproject/armada-operator/main/dev/quickstart/armada-crs.yaml",
         "kubectl get pods --namespace armada"
       ]
     },
     "uninstall": [
       {
-        "title": "Remove Armada Helm releases",
-        "description": "Uninstall all Armada components:
```bash
helm uninstall armada-server --namespace armada
helm uninstall armada-executor --namespace armada
helm uninstall armada-scheduler --namespace armada
```"
+        "title": "Remove Armada components",
+        "description": "Delete the Armada Custom Resources and namespace:
```bash
kubectl delete -n armada -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/armadaproject/armada-operator/main/dev/quickstart/armada-crs.yaml
kubectl delete namespace armada
```"
       },
       {
-        "title": "Remove the namespace",
-        "description": "Delete the namespace and all remaining resources:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace armada
```"
+        "title": "Remove Armada Operator",
+        "description": "Uninstall the operator and clean up CRDs:
```bash
helm uninstall armada-operator --namespace armada-system
kubectl delete namespace armada-system
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Remove dependencies",
+        "description": "Uninstall PostgreSQL, Redis, Pulsar, and Prometheus:
```bash
helm uninstall postgresql --namespace armada
helm uninstall redis --namespace armada
helm uninstall pulsar --namespace armada
helm uninstall kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring
kubectl delete namespace monitoring
```"
       }
     ],
     "upgrade": [
@@ -51,26 +63,38 @@
         "description": "Update the repository to get the latest chart versions:
```bash
helm repo update
```"
       },
       {
-        "title": "Upgrade Armada",
-        "description": "Upgrade the Armada server:
```bash
helm upgrade armada-server gresearch/armada --namespace armada
```"
+        "title": "Upgrade Armada Operator",
+        "description": "Upgrade the operator to the latest version:
```bash
helm upgrade armada-operator gresearch/armada-operator --namespace armada-system
```

The operator will automatically reconcile and update the managed Armada components."
       },
       {
         "title": "Verify the upgrade",
-        "description": "Check pod status after upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace armada
```"
+        "description": "Check pod status after upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace armada-system
kubectl get pods --namespace armada
```"
       }
     ],
     "troubleshooting": [
       {
-        "title": "Pods in CrashLoopBackOff",
-        "description": "Usually caused by missing or misconfigured dependencies. Check the logs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=armada-server --namespace armada
```

Verify that PostgreSQL, Redis, and Pulsar are running and accessible."
+        "title": "Operator pods not starting",
+        "description": "Check the operator logs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=armada-operator --namespace armada-system
```

Ensure cert-manager is installed if webhooks are enabled:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Armada components in CrashLoopBackOff",
+        "description": "Usually caused by missing or misconfigured dependencies. Check the logs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=armada-server --namespace armada
```

Verify that PostgreSQL, Redis, and Pulsar are running and accessible:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace armada | grep -E 'postgresql|redis|pulsar'
```"
       },
       {
         "title": "Database connection errors",
         "description": "Verify PostgreSQL is running and the connection string is correct:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace armada | grep postgresql
kubectl logs -l app=armada-server --namespace armada | grep -i database
```"
       },
       {
         "title": "Pulsar connection issues",
-        "description": "Verify Pulsar is running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace armada | grep pulsar
```"
+        "description": "Verify Pulsar is running and all components are healthy:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace armada | grep pulsar
kubectl logs -l app=armada-server --namespace armada | grep -i pulsar
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "kube-prometheus-stack not installing",
+        "description": "If you get template errors, upgrade Helm to v3.16.2 or later:
```bash
helm version
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Lookout UI not accessible",
+        "description": "Verify Lookout is running and the NodePort is exposed:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace armada | grep lookout
kubectl get pods --namespace armada | grep lookout
```"
       }
     ]
   },
@@ -80,32 +104,38 @@
       "configuration",
       "cncf",
       "app-definition",
-      "sandbox"
+      "sandbox",
+      "batch-scheduling",
+      "multi-cluster"
     ],
     "cncfProjects": [
       "armada"
     ],
     "targetResourceKinds": [
       "Deployment",
       "Namespace",
-      "Service"
+      "Service",

     ],

     "issueTypes": [
       "installation",
       "configuration"
     ],
     "installMethods": [
-      "helm"
+      "helm",

     ],
     "maturity": "sandbox",
-    "projectVersion": "v0.20.36",
+    "projectVersion": "v0.20.40",

-      "gresearch/armada-server:v0.20.36"

     ],
     "sourceUrls": {
       "docs": "https://armadaproject.io",
       "repo": "https://github.com/armadaproject/armada",
+      "operator": "https://github.com/armadaproject/armada-operator",
       "helm": "https://g-research.github.io/charts"
     },
     "qualityScore": 100
@@ -116,10 +146,10 @@
       "helm",
       "kubectl"
     ],
-    "description": "Requires a Kubernetes cluster with Helm and kubectl. Armada depends on PostgreSQL, Redis, and Apache Pulsar, which must be installed before deploying Armada."
+    "description": "Requires a Kubernetes cluster with Helm and kubectl. The Armada Operator manages the lifecycle of Armada components. External dependencies (PostgreSQL, Redis, Apache Pulsar, kube-prometheus-stack) must be installed separately."
   },
   "security": {
-    "scannedAt": "2026-03-11T15:00:00.000Z",
+    "scannedAt": "2026-04-23T06:50:00.000Z",
     "scannerVersion": "manual-review-1.0.0",
     "sanitized": true,
     "findings": []
